Jan Majcher ← Portfolio

Artistic Data Visualisation

"The good cartographer is both a scientist and an artist."

— Erwin Raisz

I am passionate about eye-catching visualisation of spatial data, using techniques like Relative Elevation Modelling, various types of hillshading and rendering. Here are some examples of my work.

01

Gulf of Naples

Open-source bathymetric data combined with ESRI World Imagery satellite basemap. Data source: CNR-ISMAR — Foglini, Rovere, Tonielli et al. (2025), Multi-resolution bathymetry of the Gulf of Naples, Earth System Science Data, CC BY 4.0 · Imagery: ESRI/ArcGIS World Imagery.

02

HMS Vanguard Shipwreck

High-resolution MBES render of HMS Vanguard, a Royal Navy dreadnought battleship lost in 1917, located near Dublin Bay, Ireland. Data source: INFOMAR project, acquired as part of my PhD research at Ulster University.

03

Relative Elevation Model — Narew River, Poland

REM visualisation of the Narew River floodplain, Poland. Relative Elevation Modelling removes the regional slope to reveal fine-scale fluvial morphology — meander scars, oxbow lakes, and channel migration patterns become visible in striking detail.

04

Relative Elevation Model — Ticino River, Italy

REM visualisation of the Ticino River, northern Italy. The braided channel morphology and historic floodplain extents are rendered with clarity against the flattened regional gradient.

05

Tremiti Island, Italy

Topographic visualisation of the Tremiti archipelago, Adriatic Sea, Italy.
